title: centaurea resupinata subsp. saguntina
intro: Centaurea resupinata subsp. saguntina, also known as Saguntum, is a subspecies of plants in the genus Centaurea, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and reducing fever. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and even menstrual cramps, due to its potential health benefits. Centaurea resupinata subsp. saguntina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
